FIESER NURSING CENTER

CCN: 26A490 · FENTON, MO 63026 · St. Louis County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
404 MAIN STREET, FENTON, MO 63026
Phone
6363434344
Certified beds
47
Avg daily residents
35 (73% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit (individual owner)
Provider type
Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2006-08-03
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 24.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.25 3.02 4.16 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.31 0.53 0.40 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.86 0.67 1.10 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.09 1.82 2.67 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.17 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.75
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.31
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.52
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.07
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.78
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-30 14 14 0 80 1 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-20 21 19 3 208 2 312
Total weighted health score
138.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (56)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
51 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. E 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly E 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. E 2025-09-30 2025-11-13
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-17 2024-11-30
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-17 2024-12-04
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-15
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. G 2024-02-20 2024-04-06
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. C 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. F 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. F 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. F 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2021-12-20 2022-02-03
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2021-12-20 2022-02-03

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.

Deficiencies 0838 — scope_severity E 0838.scope_severity
